paragraphs: Sleep is not merely a passive period of rest; it plays an active role in the consolidation of memory, a process that has been extensively examined by neuroscientists over several decades. During sleep, distinct stages—most notably rapid-eye-movement (REM) sleep and deep sleep—contribute in different ways to how memories are stored and organized. Experimental research suggests that while REM sleep strengthens emotionally charged memories, deep sleep supports the retention of factual knowledge and the acquisition of skills.

Some researchers further propose that sleep functions as a kind of neural "data management" system. While awake, the brain continuously forms new connections as it processes incoming information, creating a dense and potentially inefficient network. During sleep, however, unnecessary connections are trimmed, whereas essential ones are reinforced, a reorganization that enhances the brain's ability to retrieve information accurately.

Not surprisingly, sleep deprivation has been shown to impair memory retention and conceptual integration. Even a single night of lost sleep can reduce recall and interfere with learning. Nevertheless, studies also indicate that recovery sleep—particularly when it is deep and uninterrupted—can partially reverse these effects, suggesting that although sleep is critical for memory, the brain retains a limited capacity to adapt to disruption.

questions.question: The word "retention" in the passage is closest in meaning to

options.A: creation
options.B: replacement
options.C: transfer
options.D: preservation

questions.special_content: Experimental research suggests that while REM sleep strengthens emotionally charged memories, deep sleep supports the <mark>retention</mark> of factual knowledge and the acquisition of skills.

questions.question: REM sleep and deep sleep differ in which of the following ways?

options.A: Deep sleep is limited to the early stages of the sleep cycle, while REM sleep is not.
options.B: REM sleep is more important for skill development than deep sleep.
options.C: REM sleep involves less brain activity than deep sleep.
options.D: REM sleep and deep sleep are each linked to separate types of memory.

questions.question: The passage suggests which of the following about the effect of sleep on memory?

options.A: Sleep improves memory primarily by increasing the total number of neural connections.
options.B: Sleep improves memory by selectively eliminating some neural connections.
options.C: Sleep affects the strength of memories only during the rapid eye movement (REM) stage.
options.D: Sleep strengthens the memory of emotionally charged events but not of factual information.

questions.question: Why does the author use the phrase "Not surprisingly" to begin paragraph 3?

options.A: To suggest that the effects of sleep deprivation follow logically from earlier claims
options.B: To emphasize that people are often conscious of memory lapses after a period of poor sleep
options.C: To point out a reason for scientists' interest in establishing a link between sleep and memory
options.D: To acknowledge that sleep quality is as essential to memory retention as sleep quantity

questions.special_content: <mark>Not surprisingly</mark>, sleep deprivation has been shown to impair memory retention and conceptual integration.

questions.question:
There are four locations [■] in the passage that indicate where the following sentence could be added.

This interpretation focuses on the vital role sleep plays in helping the brain function in an optimal way.

Where would the sentence best fit? Select a location [■] to add the sentence to the passage.

options.A: Location 1
options.B: Location 2
options.C: Location 3
options.D: Location 4

questions.special_content:
...factual knowledge and the acquisition of skills. [■]
Some researchers further propose that sleep functions as a kind of neural "data management" system. [■] While awake, the brain continuously forms new connections as it processes incoming information, creating a dense and potentially inefficient network. [■] During sleep, however, unnecessary connections are trimmed, whereas essential ones are reinforced, a reorganization that enhances the brain's ability to retrieve information accurately. [■]
questions.inserted_sentence: This interpretation focuses on the vital role sleep plays in helping the brain function in an optimal way.

paragraphs: Circadian rhythms, the internal clocks regulating organisms' physiological processes, are primarily driven by light exposure. These rhythms may be disrupted by the increased artificial light of urban environments, leading to what scientists call "circadian misalignment." This misalignment not only affects sleep patterns but is linked to a higher prevalence of metabolic disorders. Interestingly, research on nocturnal animals reveals that these creatures have evolved mechanisms, such as unique melatonin production cycles, for thriving in conditions that disrupt human circadian rhythms. Such adaptations could inform potential human therapies.

Recent studies suggest that manipulating light exposure can help reset circadian clocks. Experiments demonstrated that disrupted rhythms can be realigned by the simulation of natural light cycles. However, this approach is not universally effective. Some individuals experience persistent misalignment, suggesting that other environmental or genetic factors may play significant roles.

Moreover, the factors affecting circadian regulation extend beyond light: temperature, diet, and social interactions all influence these rhythms. This complex causal web requires a multidisciplinary approach to develop comprehensive solutions. Cutting-edge research is now exploring therapeutic drugs that can rectify disruptions to the natural circadian rhythmic cycles. Whether these efforts will yield sustainable treatment options remains to be seen.

questions.question: Why does the author mention the "higher prevalence of metabolic disorders"?

options.A: To explain why circadian misalignment is increasing in urban environments
options.B: To suggest a causal relationship between metabolic illness and sleep disturbances
options.C: To support the claim that circadian misalignment affects sleep patterns
options.D: To describe one effect of the disruption of circadian rhythms by artificial light

questions.special_content: This misalignment not only affects sleep patterns but is linked to a <mark>higher prevalence of metabolic disorders</mark>.

questions.question: Why might some nocturnal animal adaptations be important for human therapies?

options.A: They provide evidence that light exposure is the greatest driver of circadian rhythms.
options.B: They offer clues about thriving in conditions that cause circadian misalignment in people.
options.C: They show that melatonin is not always effective in regulating sleep patterns.
options.D: They prove that animals' melatonin production cycles closely resemble those of humans.

questions.question: What does the author suggest about simulating natural light cycles as a way of resetting circadian clocks?

options.A: Most people experiencing circadian misalignment have seen no benefit from the treatment.
options.B: Most researchers agree that this treatment is currently the only effective approach.
options.C: The approach is most effective for people with a genetic predisposition for sleep disorders.
options.D: This approach has been successfully used to help many individuals restore their sleep patterns.

questions.question: What is the relationship between paragraph 2 and paragraph 3?

options.A: Paragraph 2 defines a concept; paragraph 3 provides more detail by discussing specific examples.
options.B: Paragraph 2 evaluates a focused approach; paragraph 3 points to broader challenges and solutions.
options.C: Paragraph 2 proposes a theory; paragraph 3 notes potential objections to it and suggests responses.
options.D: Paragraph 2 outlines a methodology; paragraph 3 reports the results of applying it to a problem.

questions.question: The word "rectify" in the passage is closest in meaning to

options.A: disclose
options.B: approve
options.C: relieve
options.D: document

questions.special_content: Cutting-edge research is now exploring therapeutic drugs that can <mark>rectify</mark> disruptions to the natural circadian rhythmic cycles.
